summary: Distant audience tape of the legendary June 9 1973 RFK show; the performance is wildly praised while the sound is flat and far‑back.
review: Fans describe this June 9 1973 Washington, D.C. concert as one of the most out‑there and memorable Dead shows, highlighting mind‑blowing interplay on “They Love Each Other/Loose Lucy” and a possible St Stephen tease in “The Greatest Story”. The recording is an audience source with a distant, flat sound that many find less than ideal, though some listeners say the ambience can mask its flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently praised (multiple 5‑star comments) while recording quality is noted as flat/distant; weighted average yields a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Distant, far‑back mic placement makes the mix sound flat and muffled; some listeners note blemishes but ambience can soften them.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans highlight the improvisational breaks and “mind‑blast” transitions, especially between They Love Each Other and Loose Lucy; overall flow is described as uniquely adventurous.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is described as being in a 'parallel universe' with Lesh during breaks.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass playing is likewise noted as existing in a parallel universe, contributing to the show’s otherworldly vibe.
